system_overview: RTSP stream capture and synchronized frame batching mechanism for AI detection.

core_components.name: LoadStreams
core_components.role: Handles RTSP network connection, video stream decoding, and raw numpy frame extraction.

core_components.name: StreamBatcher
core_components.role: Manages multi-threading (one thread per camera), thread synchronization via Condition locks, and yields synchronized frame batches to the main detector loop.

core_components.name: FrameCollector
core_components.role: Acts as a shared memory buffer. Maps frames to their source URLs and enforces a retention policy (keeping only the N most recent frames) to prevent memory leaks and latency.

workflow_steps: 1. Initialization: StreamBatcher receives a list of RTSP URLs and creates a FrameCollector.
2. Threading: A separate daemon thread is started for each camera source.
3. Extraction: Inside each thread, LoadStreams continuously pulls new frames.
4. Buffering: Threads push new frames into the shared FrameCollector and trigger a Condition notification.
5. Synchronization: The main StreamBatcher iterator waits until the batch is ready (frames present from all sources, or >1 frame from any source to prevent blocking).
6. Yielding: Once ready, it takes a snapshot of the current frames, clears the buffer, and yields the batch to the AI model.
